item.text: I’ll inspect the fixture manifest, available bomly tooling, and the test harness first, then apply only dependency-related changes and document each vulnerable package in `FIXES.md`.

item.aggregated_output:
# The reactor's tests need external infrastructure, so this compiles and
# builds the modules without running them. -DskipTests (NOT
# -Dmaven.test.skip): the `grouper` module depends on grouperClient's
# test-jar, which -Dmaven.test.skip would not build.
cd fixtures/bigapp/grouper-parent && mvn -B -DskipTests install

item.text: The initial scan target was too broad for bomly’s executor, but discovery confirms the Maven projects. I’m narrowing the scan to the reactor parent that `make test` actually builds, then I’ll drill into each reported package before changing versions.

item.text: Bomly reports 21 vulnerable packages in the built reactor, with the compact result truncated. I’m writing the full scan JSON to a temporary file so I can enumerate every vulnerability/package instead of relying on the capped summary.

/usr/local/bin/bomly
Scan dependencies and render a graph or SBOM

Usage:
  bomly scan [flags]

Examples:
  bomly scan --enrich --audit
  bomly scan -o spdx=bomly.spdx.json
  bomly scan --url https://github.com/bomly-dev/bomly-cli --ref main --json
  bomly scan --image alpine:3.20

Flags:
      --allow-license stringArray            Allowed SPDX license identifier or expression. Repeatable
      --allow-vulnerability-id stringArray   Vulnerability ID to ignore during policy evaluation. Repeatable
      --analyze                              [Experimental] Run code analysis to confirm whether vulnerabilities are reachable from application code
      --analyzers string                     Reachability analyzer selectors. Use names. Prefix with '+' to append defaults or '-' to remove from defaults
      --audit                                Evaluate policy and create findings from package vulnerability data
      --auditors string                      Auditor selectors. Use names or aliases. Prefix with '+' to append defaults or '-' to remove from defaults
      --deny-group stringArray               Package URL namespace to deny. Repeatable
      --deny-license stringArray             Denied SPDX license identifier or expression. Repeatable
      --deny-package stringArray             Package URL to deny. Repeatable
      --detectors string                     Detector selectors. Use names or aliases. Prefix with '+' to append defaults or '-' to remove from defaults
      --ecosystems string                    Ecosystems to use; supports +name/-name to add/remove from all
      --enrich                               Enrich packages with external license and vulnerability data
      --fail-on stringArray                  Constraint(s) for which findings should be created. Repeatable; constraints AND together. Severity: any|low|medium|high|critical. Reachability: reachable. Exploitability: exploitable. Example: --fail-on low --fail-on reachable
  -f, --format string                        Output format: text, json, markdown, sarif, spdx, cyclonedx (SBOM formats are scan-only)
  -h, --help                                 help for scan
      --image string                         Container image reference to scan with Syft
      --install-arg stringArray              Additional detector-specific install argument; may be repeated
      --install-first                        Run detector-specific dependency installation before resolving graphs
      --interactive                          Open an interactive terminal UI
      --json                                 Shortcut for --format json
      --license-exempt-package stringArray   Package URL exempt from license policy checks. Repeatable
      --matchers string                      Matcher selectors. Use names or aliases. Prefix with '+' to append defaults or '-' to remove from defaults
  -o, --output stringArray                   Additional output target as <format> or <format>=<path>; repeat for multiple outputs. Supports text, json, markdown, sarif, spdx, cyclonedx
      --path string                          Execution target path
      --protected-package stringArray        Canonical package name to protect from typosquatting. Repeatable
      --ref string                           Git reference to scan when using --url
      --sbom                                 Treat the selected filesystem target as an SBOM file
      --scope string                         Filter dependencies by scope: runtime or development
      --typosquat-mode string                Typosquatting policy mode: warn or fail
      --typosquat-threshold string           Similarity threshold for typosquatting detection
      --url string                           Git repository URL to clone and scan
      --warn-only                            Downgrade failing findings to warnings

Global Flags:
      --config string   YAML config file path
  -q, --quiet           Suppress non-error stderr output
  -v, --verbose count   Increase verbosity (-v = info, -vv = debug)

Explore available detectors, matchers, and auditors with `bomly plugins list`.

item.text: There are 21 vulnerable package coordinates in the reactor. Some are direct properties, and others are old transitives from Axis2, AWS SDK, Qpid, Box SDK, Google client, and MySQL; I’m asking bomly for fix context on the remaining packages before pinning.

item.text: I have the fix set now: update direct managed properties where they exist, add dependency-management overrides for vulnerable transitives, and document the three bomly `no-fix-upstream` packages separately. I’m editing only `grouper-parent/pom.xml` and adding `FIXES.md`.

item.text: A few dependencies have explicit versions in child modules, so parent dependency management alone would not affect them. I’m updating those explicit vulnerable coordinates where a fixed version exists, and using exclusions plus the already managed Bouncy Castle `jdk18on` artifacts where the old `jdk15on` artifacts have no fixed release in that artifact line.
The dependency edits are in place. I’m running a Maven build check next; if any override is incompatible or unavailable, I’ll adjust before documenting.

item.text: The rescan reduced vulnerable packages from 21 to 5. The remaining five are all reported by bomly as `no_fix_upstream`, including one remaining `jackson-databind` advisory after the fixed-version upgrade; I’m recording that distinction in `FIXES.md`.

item.command: /bin/bash -lc "bomly scan --path fixtures/bigapp/grouper-parent --enrich --audit --format json -o json=/tmp/bomly-grouper-after.json >/tmp/bomly-grouper-after.out 2>/tmp/bomly-grouper-after.err; node -e \"const d=require('/tmp/bomly-grouper-after.json'); const rows=[...new Map(d.findings.filter(f=>f.kind==='vulnerability').map(f=>[[f.package.name,f.package.version,f.id].join('\\t'),f])).values()].sort((a,b)=>(a.package.name+a.id).localeCompare(b.package.name+b.id)); for (const f of rows) console.log([f.package.name,f.package.version,f.id,f.severity,f.title].join('\\t'))\""

/bin/bash -lc "bomly scan --path fixtures/bigapp/grouper-parent --enrich --audit --format json -o json=/tmp/bomly-grouper-after.json >/tmp/bomly-grouper-after.out 2>/tmp/bomly-grouper-after.err; node -e \"const d=require('/tmp/bomly-grouper-after.json'); const rows=[...new Map(d.findings.filter(f=>f.kind==='vulnerability').map(f=>[[f.package.name,f.package.version,f.id].join('\\t'),f])).values()].sort((a,b)=>(a.package.name+a.id).localeCompare(b.package.name+b.id)); for (const f of rows) console.log([f.package.name,f.package.version,f.id,f.severity,f.title].join('\\t'))\""

item.text: I’m adding `FIXES.md` with one package entry for every vulnerable coordinate bomly identified, including the remaining no-fixed-version cases and the reason each changed version/override was chosen.
`FIXES.md` is added. I’m running the required root `make test` now; this is the real build gate for the fixture.

`make test` is still running. It has compiled through the UI and is in the WS module; the checkstyle parser error shown is pre-existing-style output with `failOnViolation=false`, so it has not stopped the build.
